## 3. Validation Files

### 3a. Core Validation Logic

**`pkg/apis/networking/validation/validation.go`**

#### `IngressValidationOptions` struct (L253–259)
```go
type IngressValidationOptions struct {
    // AllowInvalidSecretName: true on update if existing object already has invalid secret name
    AllowInvalidSecretName bool
    // AllowInvalidWildcardHostRule: true on update if existing object has invalid wildcard rules
    AllowInvalidWildcardHostRule bool
}
```
This struct is the mechanism for **graduated validation** — stricter on create, permissive on update for pre-existing invalid data.

#### Public entry points

| Function | L# | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ValidateIngressCreate(ingress)` | L269 | Strict: both opts=false; also validates annotation vs `spec.ingressClassName` consistency |
| `ValidateIngressUpdate(ingress, old)` | L285 | Permissive: opts computed from old object via helpers |
| `ValidateIngressStatusUpdate(ingress, old)` | L348 | Only validates ObjectMeta + LoadBalancer status |
| `ValidateIngressLoadBalancerStatus(status, fldPath)` | L355 | Validates IP (valid IP) and Hostname (DNS subdomain, not an IP) |

#### Internal validation chain

```
ValidateIngressCreate / ValidateIngressUpdate
  └─ validateIngress(ingress, opts)                    L262
       ├─ ValidateObjectMeta
       └─ ValidateIngressSpec(spec, fldPath, opts)     L324
            ├─ validateIngressBackend(defaultBackend)  L464
            ├─ validateIngressRules(rules, opts)       L374
            │    └─ validateIngressRuleValue           L406
            │         └─ validateHTTPIngressRuleValue  L414
            │              └─ validateHTTPIngressPath  L425  ← pathType enforcement
            └─ validateIngressTLS(spec, opts)          L296
```

#### `validateHTTPIngressPath` — the pathType enforcement point (L425–461)

```go
func validateHTTPIngressPath(path *networking.HTTPIngressPath, fldPath *field.Path, opts IngressValidationOptions) field.ErrorList {
    if path.PathType == nil {
        // L428-430: REQUIRED — returns immediately with error
        return append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child("pathType"), "pathType must be specified"))
    }
    switch *path.PathType {
    case networking.PathTypeExact, networking.PathTypePrefix:
        // path must start with "/"
        // path must not contain "//" or "/./"
        // path must not end with "/." or "/.."
    case networking.PathTypeImplementationSpecific:
        // path, if non-empty, must start with "/"
    default:
        // field.NotSupported error
    }
    // then validateIngressBackend
}
```

This single nil-check at L428 is the **critical difference** between v1beta1 and v1 behavior: in v1beta1, `pathType` is defaulted to `ImplementationSpecific` before validation runs, so it is never nil. In v1, there is no defaulting, so a missing `pathType` fails here.

#### Backward-compatibility helpers (L621–650)

```go
// allowInvalidSecretName: returns true if any existing TLS entry has an invalid secret name
func allowInvalidSecretName(oldIngress *networking.Ingress) bool { ... }

// allowInvalidWildcardHostRule: returns true if any existing wildcard-host rule is already invalid
func allowInvalidWildcardHostRule(oldIngress *networking.Ingress) bool { ... }
```

These allow objects that were created with looser validation (before stricter rules were added) to continue to be updated without being rejected.

### 3b. Validation Tests

**`pkg/apis/networking/validation/validation_test.go`**
- `TestValidateIngressCreate` — exercises `ValidateIngressCreate` including pathType=nil rejection, annotation conflicts, backend validation
- `TestValidateIngressUpdate` — exercises backward-compat options (invalid secret names allowed on update if pre-existing)

## 4. Defaulting Files

### 4a. `networking/v1beta1` defaults

**`pkg/apis/networking/v1beta1/defaults.go`** (L28–33)
```go
func SetDefaults_HTTPIngressPath(obj *networkingv1beta1.HTTPIngressPath) {
    var defaultPathType = networkingv1beta1.PathTypeImplementationSpecific
    if obj.PathType == nil {
        obj.PathType = &defaultPathType
    }
}
```
This is the **key migration mechanism**: when a v1beta1 Ingress is decoded (e.g., from a client that didn't set `pathType`), this defaulter fires and sets `ImplementationSpecific`. The object then passes validation because `pathType` is no longer nil.

**`pkg/apis/networking/v1beta1/zz_generated.defaults.go`** (L38–55)
```go
func SetObjectDefaults_Ingress(in *networkingv1beta1.Ingress) {
    for i := range in.Spec.Rules {
        if a.IngressRuleValue.HTTP != nil {
            for j := range a.IngressRuleValue.HTTP.Paths {
                SetDefaults_HTTPIngressPath(&Paths[j])  // calls the above
            }
        }
    }
}
// Registered via: scheme.AddTypeDefaultingFunc(&networkingv1beta1.Ingress{}, ...)
```

### 4b. `extensions/v1beta1` defaults

**`pkg/apis/extensions/v1beta1/defaults.go`** (L165–170)
```go
func SetDefaults_HTTPIngressPath(obj *extensionsv1beta1.HTTPIngressPath) {
    var defaultPathType = extensionsv1beta1.PathTypeImplementationSpecific
    if obj.PathType == nil {
        obj.PathType = &defaultPathType
    }
}
```

**`pkg/apis/extensions/v1beta1/zz_generated.defaults.go`** (L666–684)

### 4c. `networking/v1` defaults

**`pkg/apis/networking/v1/defaults.go`**
- Contains only `SetDefaults_IngressClass` (for IngressClass scope defaulting)
- **No `SetDefaults_HTTPIngressPath`** — intentional. In v1, `pathType` is required; there is nothing to default.

**`pkg/apis/networking/v1/zz_generated.defaults.go`**
- No Ingress defaulting registered.

## 5. Registry / Strategy Files

### 5a. Strategy

**`pkg/registry/networking/ingress/strategy.go`**

Two strategy types:

**`ingressStrategy`** (main write strategy):
| Method | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `NamespaceScoped()` | `true` |
| `GetResetFields()` | Resets `status` for all 3 API versions: `extensions/v1beta1`, `networking.k8s.io/v1beta1`, `networking.k8s.io/v1` |
| `PrepareForCreate` | Clears `Status`, sets `Generation = 1` |
| `PrepareForUpdate` | Preserves `Status` from old; increments `Generation` if `Spec` changed |
| `Validate` | Calls `validation.ValidateIngressCreate(ingress)` |
| `ValidateUpdate` | Calls `validation.ValidateIngressUpdate(new, old)` |
| `WarningsOnCreate` | Warns if `kubernetes.io/ingress.class` annotation is set without `spec.ingressClassName` |
| `AllowCreateOnUpdate()` | `false` |
| `AllowUnconditionalUpdate()` | `true` |

**`ingressStatusStrategy`** (status subresource strategy):
| Method | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `GetResetFields()` | Resets `spec` for all 3 API versions |
| `PrepareForUpdate` | Preserves `Spec` from old (status update cannot change spec) |
| `ValidateUpdate` | Calls `validation.ValidateIngressStatusUpdate(new, old)` |

The `GetResetFields` covering all three API versions is the **server-side apply field management** mechanism — it tells the apply machinery which fields are owned by the server (not the user) for each version.

### 5b. Storage

**`pkg/registry/networking/ingress/storage/storage.go`**

```go
func NewREST(optsGetter generic.RESTOptionsGetter) (*REST, *StatusREST, error) {
    store := &genericregistry.Store{
        NewFunc:                   func() runtime.Object { return &networking.Ingress{} },
        NewListFunc:               func() runtime.Object { return &networking.IngressList{} },
        DefaultQualifiedResource:  networking.Resource("ingresses"),
        SingularQualifiedResource: networking.Resource("ingress"),
        CreateStrategy:      ingress.Strategy,
        UpdateStrategy:      ingress.Strategy,
        DeleteStrategy:      ingress.Strategy,
        ResetFieldsStrategy: ingress.Strategy,
        TableConvertor: ...,
    }
    // statusStore uses ingress.StatusStrategy for UpdateStrategy and ResetFieldsStrategy
}

func (r *REST) ShortNames() []string { return []string{"ing"} }
```

### 5c. REST Storage Wiring

**`pkg/registry/networking/rest/storage_settings.go`**

```go
func (p RESTStorageProvider) NewRESTStorage(...) (genericapiserver.APIGroupInfo, error) {
    // Version priority map populated:
    // v1alpha1 storage → networkingapiv1beta1.SchemeGroupVersion.Version ("v1beta1")
    // v1 storage       → networkingapiv1.SchemeGroupVersion.Version ("v1")
}

func (p RESTStorageProvider) v1Storage(...) (map[string]rest.Storage, error) {
    // "networkpolicies" → networkpolicystore
    // "ingresses"       → ingressstore.NewREST  ← Ingress REST + StatusREST
    // "ingressclasses"  → ingressclassstore
}
```

Note: `v1beta1` Ingress is **not** registered in `v1alpha1Storage` or any separate v1beta1 storage map. The single `ingressstore.NewREST` serves all versions via conversion. The `networking.k8s.io/v1beta1` group version is reused for `ipaddresses` and `servicecidrs` (newer alpha resources), not for Ingress.

## 6. Scheme Registration / Install

**`pkg/apis/networking/install/install.go`**
```go
func Install(scheme *runtime.Scheme) {
    utilruntime.Must(networking.AddToScheme(scheme))   // internal types
    utilruntime.Must(v1.AddToScheme(scheme))           // networking/v1
    utilruntime.Must(v1beta1.AddToScheme(scheme))      // networking/v1beta1
    utilruntime.Must(v1alpha1.AddToScheme(scheme))     // networking/v1alpha1
    // Version priority: v1 > v1beta1 > v1alpha1
    utilruntime.Must(scheme.SetVersionPriority(v1.SchemeGroupVersion, v1beta1.SchemeGroupVersion, v1alpha1.SchemeGroupVersion))
}
```

The version priority determines which version is used for serialization when no explicit version is requested. `v1` wins, confirming it is the storage version.

**`pkg/apis/networking/v1beta1/register.go`** — calls `addDefaultingFuncs` which calls `RegisterDefaults`, which registers `SetObjectDefaults_Ingress` for `networking/v1beta1`.

## 7. Storage Version Migration Evidence

**`test/integration/etcd/data.go`** (L197–201)
```go
gvr("networking.k8s.io", "v1", "ingresses"): {
    Stub:             `{"metadata": {"name": "ingress3"}, "spec": {"defaultBackend": {"service":{"name":"service", "port":{"number": 5000}}}}}`,
    ExpectedEtcdPath: "/registry/ingress/" + namespace + "/ingress3",
},
```

This integration test fixture confirms:
- **Storage version**: `networking.k8s.io/v1` (the stub uses v1 field names: `defaultBackend`, `service.port.number`)
- **etcd key prefix**: `/registry/ingress/<namespace>/<name>` (not `/registry/ingresses/`)
- **No `pathType` in stub**: valid because the stub uses `defaultBackend` only (no rules/paths), so the pathType-required validation is not triggered

---

## 8. The `pathType` Beta→GA Migration: End-to-End Flow

This is the central design story connecting all the files above:

```
Client sends v1beta1 Ingress (pathType omitted)
    │
    ▼
Defaulter fires (networking/v1beta1/defaults.go:SetDefaults_HTTPIngressPath)
    → pathType = "ImplementationSpecific"
    │
    ▼
Conversion: v1beta1 → internal (networking/v1beta1/conversion.go)
    → ServiceName/ServicePort → Service.Name/Service.Port
    → Backend → DefaultBackend
    │
    ▼
Strategy.Validate → ValidateIngressCreate (networking/validation/validation.go)
    → validateHTTPIngressPath: pathType != nil ✓ (was defaulted)
    │
    ▼
Conversion: internal → v1 (networking/v1/zz_generated.conversion.go)
    → autoConvert_networking_IngressSpec_To_v1_IngressSpec (1:1 copy)
    │
    ▼
Stored in etcd as networking.k8s.io/v1 at /registry/ingress/<ns>/<name>

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Client sends v1 Ingress (pathType omitted)
    │
    ▼
No defaulter for v1 Ingress paths
    │
    ▼
Conversion: v1 → internal (1:1, no structural change)
    │
    ▼
Strategy.Validate → ValidateIngressCreate
    → validateHTTPIngressPath: pathType == nil → field.Required error ✗
    │
    ▼
Request rejected: "pathType must be specified"
```

The `answer.json` has been written to `/workspace/answer.json`. It is valid JSON with 24 files catalogued across 5 top-level sections:

### What the file contains

| Section | Contents |
|---|---|
| `summary` | One-sentence description of the task |

| `files` | Deeply structured breakdown of every file by category: internal types, manual conversion, generated conversion, validation, defaulting, registry/strategy, REST wiring, scheme install, storage version evidence |
| `pathType_migration_flow` | Step-by-step traces of three scenarios: v1beta1 client omits pathType (succeeds via defaulter), v1 client omits pathType (rejected at L428), and update backward-compat (invalid secret name allowed) |
| `complete_file_list` | Flat list of all 24 files with category tags for easy lookup |

### Key findings captured

1. **Conversion** — Two sets of manual converters (one for `extensions/v1beta1`, one for `networking/v1beta1`) handle the structural mismatch between the flat beta backend (`ServiceName string` + `ServicePort intstr`) and the internal nested backend (`Service *IngressServiceBackend`). The v1 conversion is entirely generated because v1 and internal shapes match.

2. **pathType enforcement** — The single enforcement point is `validateHTTPIngressPath` at line 428–430 of `validation.go`. The nil-check fires for v1 (no defaulter) but not for v1beta1 (defaulter sets `ImplementationSpecific` before validation).

3. **Graduated validation** — `IngressValidationOptions` with `AllowInvalidSecretName` and `AllowInvalidWildcardHostRule` flags, computed from the old object on update, allows pre-existing invalid objects to continue being updated.

4. **Storage version migration** — `install.go` sets version priority `v1 > v1beta1 > v1alpha1`; the etcd integration test confirms objects are stored at `/registry/ingress/<ns>/<name>` in v1 format; a single `ingressstore.NewREST` serves all three API versions via conversion.

arguments.message:
The task is complete. `/workspace/answer.json` has been written with a comprehensive, structured catalogue of all 24 Go source files in `kubernetes/kubernetes` that implement Ingress API handling for both `v1beta1` and `v1`.

## What was delivered

The JSON file covers five top-level sections:

### 1. Architecture
- Hub-and-spoke conversion model with `pkg/apis/networking` as the internal hub
- Storage version: `networking.k8s.io/v1`, etcd key: `/registry/ingress/<namespace>/<name>`

### 2. Files (deeply structured by category)

**Internal types** (`pkg/apis/networking/types.go`)
- `PathType`, `PathTypeExact/Prefix/ImplementationSpecific`, `HTTPIngressPath` (with `*PathType`), `IngressBackend`, `IngressServiceBackend`

**Manual conversion** (2 files)
- `pkg/apis/extensions/v1beta1/conversion.go` — 4 functions mapping flat `ServiceName`/`ServicePort` ↔ nested `Service *IngressServiceBackend`; `Backend` ↔ `DefaultBackend`
- `pkg/apis/networking/v1beta1/conversion.go` — identical logic, different import

**Generated conversion** (3 files)
- `extensions/v1beta1/zz_generated.conversion.go` — top-level Ingress, IngressTLS
- `networking/v1beta1/zz_generated.conversion.go` — adds IngressLoadBalancerIngress; `autoConvert_*IngressSpec*` drops `Backend` field (handled manually)
- `networking/v1/zz_generated.conversion.go` — almost entirely generated (v1 shape matches internal); `Convert_v1_IngressSpec_To_networking_IngressSpec` is a thin wrapper

**Validation** (`pkg/apis/networking/validation/validation.go`)
- `IngressValidationOptions` struct with `AllowInvalidSecretName` + `AllowInvalidWildcardHostRule` for graduated validation
- `ValidateIngressCreate` (strict opts), `ValidateIngressUpdate` (permissive opts from old object)
- `validateHTTPIngressPath` lines 428–430: **the single pathType nil-check** — returns `field.Required` immediately if `pathType == nil`
- `allowInvalidSecretName` / `allowInvalidWildcardHostRule` backward-compat helpers

**Defaulting** (5 files)
- `networking/v1beta1/defaults.go` + generated: `SetDefaults_HTTPIngressPath` → `ImplementationSpecific` when nil
- `extensions/v1beta1/defaults.go` + generated: same for extensions group
- `networking/v1/defaults.go`: **no Ingress path defaulting** (intentional — pathType is required in v1)

**Registry/Strategy** (`pkg/registry/networking/ingress/strategy.go`)
- `ingressStrategy.GetResetFields`: resets `status` for all 3 API versions (`extensions/v1beta1`, `networking.k8s.io/v1beta1`, `networking.k8s.io/v1`)
- `ingressStatusStrategy.GetResetFields`: resets `spec` for all 3 versions
- `Validate` → `ValidateIngressCreate`; `ValidateUpdate` → `ValidateIngressUpdate`
- `WarningsOnCreate`: deprecation warning for `kubernetes.io/ingress.class` annotation

**Storage** (`pkg/registry/networking/ingress/storage/storage.go`)
- `NewREST` returns `*REST` (short name `"ing"`) + `*StatusREST`; single store serves all 3 API versions via conversion

**REST wiring** (`pkg/registry/networking/rest/storage_settings.go`)
- `v1Storage` registers `"ingresses"` under `networking.k8s.io/v1` only; v1beta1 requests served via conversion from this single store

**Scheme install** (`pkg/apis/networking/install/install.go`)
- Version priority: `v1 > v1beta1 > v1alpha1`

**Storage version evidence** (`test/integration/etcd/data.go`)
- Confirms v1 as storage version; etcd path `/registry/ingress/<ns>/<name>`

### 3. pathType Migration Flow
Step-by-step traces for: v1beta1 client omits pathType (succeeds via defaulter → ImplementationSpecific), v1 client omits pathType (rejected at validation L428), and update backward-compat (invalid secret name tolerated).

### 4. Complete file list
Flat list of all 24 files with category tags.
